title: tool_intercept: bash completion hint regresses on outputs containing word 'error'
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
kiss
## Location
src/tool_intercept.rs:717-722 (format_tool_results_xml_with_names)
## Violation
```rust
"bash" | "Bash" => {
    if content.contains("error") || content.contains("Error") || content.contains("failed") {
        None // Don't add completion hint for errors
    } else {
        Some("Command executed successfully.")
    }
}
```

`String::contains` matches anywhere in the buffer. Many valid bash successes contain the word "error" (`grep -c error logfile`, `find . -name '*_error_*'`, `cargo build` output that says "0 errors"). The hint is suppressed for these. Conversely, a real failure that prints "fault" or "abort" gets the success hint anyway.

This conflates "process exit code" with "string content of stdout" — they are independent signals.

## Mandated refactor
Use the actual `is_error` field that has already been computed for `*is_error in results` (it is the function parameter the outer `if *is_error` branch already uses). Remove the substring heuristic entirely.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
